old_value:
{"id":"iv-pxid","title":"Plugin publishing pipeline: structural validation + fix undeclared features","description":"Audit found systemic issues in plugin publishing pipeline. interbump.sh handles version numbers but never validates plugin structure, allowing broken plugins to ship.

**Critical findings:**
- 14 plugins have hooks on disk that Claude Code never loads (missing hooks key in plugin.json)
- 7 plugins declare zero features despite having skills/commands on disk
- 3 version mismatches (interflux, interlock, interkasten)
- 1 hardcoded API key (interject EXA_API_KEY)
- 106 stale cache directories (76.8% bloat)

**Work items:**
1. Create validate-plugin.sh — structural validator (undeclared hooks/skills/commands, secret scanning)
2. Wire validation into interbump.sh as pre-publish gate
3. Fix 14 undeclared hooks across affected plugins
4. Fix 7 zero-feature plugins (add missing declarations)
5. Fix 3 version mismatches + hardcoded API key
6. Cache cleanup tooling

**Affected plugins (hooks):** intercheck, interfluence, interflux, interject, interkasten, interlearn, interline, interlock, intermem, intermux, interserve, interstat, tool-time
**Affected plugins (zero features):** interline, interpath, interphase, interwatch, tool-time, tuivision
**Version drift:** interflux (0.2.20 vs marketplace 0.2.19), interlock (0.2.2 vs 0.2.1), interkasten (0.4.3 vs marketplace 0.4.4)

Full audit: interverse/intersynth/docs/research/audit-plugin-version-mismatches.md","notes":"Sprint started, brainstorm phase complete. Next: strategy. Brainstorm doc written, audit report available.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T21:18:30Z","created_by":"mk","updated_at":"2026-02-23T07:31:03Z","closed_at":"2026-02-23T07:31:03Z","close_reason":"Closed","work_type":"mutex"}

{"id":"iv-t217","title":"Implement dirty row tracking in Bigend TUI","description":"Port FrankenTUI's three-tier dirty tracking system (row bitmap -\u003e dirty spans -\u003e cell bitmap) to Bigend's Go/Bubble Tea renderer. Currently Bigend re-renders entire frames every tick. Even a simple dirty-row boolean vector could skip 90%+ of diff work on stable frames. Reference: research/frankentui/ buffer.rs dirty_rows implementation. See apps/autarch/docs/research/frankentui-research-synthesis.md Tier 1 #1.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T08:32:36Z","created_by":"mk","updated_at":"2026-02-23T16:28:57Z","closed_at":"2026-02-23T16:28:57Z","close_reason":"Section-level render caching shipped","work_type":"mutex"}

{"id":"iv-omzb","title":"Implement inline mode with scrollback preservation in Bigend","description":"Port FrankenTUI's inline mode protocol to Bigend for agent output display. TerminalWriter protocol: save cursor, clear UI region, render, restore cursor. Logs write above UI and terminal scrolls naturally. Two atomic write points: write_log() for safe background thread writes above UI, and present_ui() for main thread frame rendering. This is exactly what we need for agent output in Bigend. Reference: research/frankentui/ terminal_writer.rs. See apps/autarch/docs/research/frankentui-research-synthesis.md Tier 1 #3.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T08:32:37Z","created_by":"mk","updated_at":"2026-02-23T20:16:04Z","closed_at":"2026-02-23T20:16:04Z","close_reason":"Shipped: LogHandler, LogPane, scrollback dump, panic recovery in standalone Bigend","work_type":"mutex"}

TerminalWriter protocol: save cursor, clear UI region, render, restore cursor. Logs write above UI and terminal scrolls naturally. Two atomic write points: write_log() for safe background thread writes above UI, and present_ui() for main thread frame rendering. This is exactly what we need for agent output in Bigend. Reference: research/frankentui/ terminal_writer.rs. See apps/autarch/docs/research/frankentui-research-synthesis.md Tier 1 #3.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T08:32:37Z","created_by":"mk","updated_at":"2026-02-23T20:16:04Z","closed_at":"2026-02-23T20:16:04Z","close_reason":"Shipped: LogHandler, LogPane, scrollback dump, panic recovery in standalone Bigend","work_type":"mutex"}

{"id":"iv-exos","title":"Research: bias-aware product decision framework","description":"Research area #10. LLM judges show 40-57% systematic bias rates (position, verbosity, authority, anchoring, omission, framing, status quo). Clavain's brainstorm/strategy phases use LLM judgment for product decisions but have no bias mitigation. Deliverable: review agent or gate that tests for bias categories and flags high-risk product decisions for human escalation. Sources: ACL 2025 survey, OpenReview, PNAS.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-14T09:07:09Z","created_by":"mk","updated_at":"2026-02-14T09:07:09Z","work_type":"mutex"}

old_value:
{"id":"iv-6i37","title":"Blueprint distillation: channel optimization for sprint intake","description":"DeepCode (arxiv:2512.07921) treats code synthesis as a channel optimization problem. Blueprint Distillation compresses high-entropy source documents (PRDs, brainstorms, specs) into structured, high-signal implementation blueprints. Filters narrative noise. Extracts critical constraints.

Result: 75.9% on 3-paper evaluation, surpassing PhD-level human experts (72.4%).

Application to Clavain sprint workflow: The brainstorm → strategy → plan pipeline already does ad-hoc distillation. Formalizing this as an explicit 'blueprint' step could:
1. Reduce tokens in the plan phase (compressed input)
2. Improve plan quality (noise-filtered constraints)
3. Enable reuse across sessions (blueprint is cacheable)

DeepCode's 4 operations map to Clavain: Blueprint Distillation → brainstorm/strategy, Stateful Code Memory → beads + interkasten, Conditional RAG → tldr-swinton, Error Correction → flux-drive reviews.

Effort: ~3 days to prototype in the strategy skill.","notes":"Related: iv-cu5w (Tiered Brainstorming), iv-1zh2.4 (artifact-first handoffs). Could be integrated into strategy skill as an explicit blueprint extraction step.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-15T17:31:10Z","created_by":"mk","updated_at":"2026-02-15T17:31:23Z","work_type":"mutex"}

{"id":"iv-444d","title":"Catalog-reminder → Interwatch escalation","description":"When catalog-reminder.sh fires on component file changes (skills/commands/agents added or removed), also write a component_count_changed signal for Interwatch. This connects the existing catalog-reminder hook to Interwatch's drift scoring (weight 3 for PRD), eliminating the gap where component count changes aren't reflected in drift scores.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-14T11:31:27Z","created_by":"mk","updated_at":"2026-02-20T06:11:32Z","work_type":"mutex"}

{"id":"iv-yeka","title":"[clavain] Update roadmap.md for new vision + parallel tracks","description":"Rewrite hub/clavain/docs/roadmap.md to reflect the new vision. Replace old Phase 1/2/3 with three parallel tracks: Track A (Kernel Integration): A1 hook cutover, A2 sprint→kernel-driven, A3 event-driven phase advancement. Track B (Model Routing): B1 static phase→model table, B2 complexity-aware routing, B3 adaptive via Interspect. Track C (Agency Architecture): C1 agency specs (declarative per-stage config), C2 agent fleet registry (capability+cost profiles), C3 composer (matches specs to fleet with budget constraint), C4 cross-phase handoff + result aggregation, C5 self-building loop. Update companion constellation table. Update research agenda to align with new vision.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:08:41Z","created_by":"mk","updated_at":"2026-02-20T03:30:06Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T03:30:06Z","close_reason":"Roadmap updated: E3 done, vision epic closed, bead stats refreshed (1000 total), version 0.6.43. Three-track structure preserved with A1 marked complete.","work_type":"mutex"}

new_value: {"notes":"FLUX-DRIVE REVIEW FINDING: Dependency on 'empirical testing of nested Task behavior' is likely unnecessary. Pattern is proven in OpenHands AgentDelegateAction and MASAI architecture. Can be prototyped directly using existing file indirection + Task tool. However, fd-user-product warns this is invisible infrastructure — won't improve UX unless context limits are actually being hit. Measure first (how often does flux-drive hit 150K+ tokens?).\n[doc-map] plan (declared): docs/plans/2026-02-19-hierarchical-dispatch-meta-agent.md"}
old_value:
{"id":"iv-quk4","title":"Hierarchical dispatch: meta-agent for N-agent fan-out","description":"Instead of orchestrator spawning N subagents (N tool call results in parent context), spawn one meta-agent that internally launches N workers. Parent sees 1 result, not N. Potential 98% parent context savings. Critical unknown: notification bubbling behavior — do grandchild agent notifications bubble to grandparent? Needs empirical testing.

Ref patterns: AgentOrchestra (arxiv:2506.12508), TodoEvolve (arxiv:2602.07839), Clavain brainstorm doc 2026-02-14-agent-dispatch-context-optimization.md.

Implementation: flux-drive skill could dispatch a single 'meta-reviewer' agent that reads the dispatch manifest and launches all fd-* agents internally. The meta-reviewer then synthesizes and returns a single structured report.

Effort: ~3 days for prototype + empirical testing of notification/context behavior.
Depends on: empirical testing of Claude Code nested Task behavior.","notes":"FLUX-DRIVE REVIEW FINDING: Dependency on 'empirical testing of nested Task behavior' is likely unnecessary. Pattern is proven in OpenHands AgentDelegateAction and MASAI architecture. Can be prototyped directly using existing file indirection + Task tool. However, fd-user-product warns this is invisible infrastructure — won't improve UX unless context limits are actually being hit. Measure first (how often does flux-drive hit 150K+ tokens?).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-15T17:31:10Z","created_by":"mk","updated_at":"2026-02-15T17:41:50Z","work_type":"mutex"}

{"id":"iv-pxid","title":"Plugin publishing pipeline: structural validation + fix undeclared features","description":"Audit found systemic issues in plugin publishing pipeline. interbump.sh handles version numbers but never validates plugin structure, allowing broken plugins to ship.

**Critical findings:**
- 14 plugins have hooks on disk that Claude Code never loads (missing hooks key in plugin.json)
- 7 plugins declare zero features despite having skills/commands on disk
- 3 version mismatches (interflux, interlock, interkasten)
- 1 hardcoded API key (interject EXA_API_KEY)
- 106 stale cache directories (76.8% bloat)

**Work items:**
1. Create validate-plugin.sh — structural validator (undeclared hooks/skills/commands, secret scanning)
2. Wire validation into interbump.sh as pre-publish gate
3. Fix 14 undeclared hooks across affected plugins
4. Fix 7 zero-feature plugins (add missing declarations)
5. Fix 3 version mismatches + hardcoded API key
6. Cache cleanup tooling

**Affected plugins (hooks):** intercheck, interfluence, interflux, interject, interkasten, interlearn, interline, interlock, intermem, intermux, interserve, interstat, tool-time
**Affected plugins (zero features):** interline, interpath, interphase, interwatch, tool-time, tuivision
**Version drift:** interflux (0.2.20 vs marketplace 0.2.19), interlock (0.2.2 vs 0.2.1), interkasten (0.4.3 vs marketplace 0.4.4)

Full audit: interverse/intersynth/docs/research/audit-plugin-version-mismatches.md","notes":"Sprint started, brainstorm phase complete. Next: strategy. Brainstorm doc written, audit report available.

{"id":"iv-ho3","title":"Epic: StrongDM Factory Substrate — validation-first infrastructure for Clavain","description":"Autarch becomes the runtime backend for Clavain's StrongDM-inspired factory techniques. Clavain (Claude Code plugin) handles prompt engineering and orchestration; Autarch handles persistent state, background execution, isolation, and observability.

Key capabilities to add:
1. Scenario Bank + Satisfaction Scoring (Gurgeh scenarios + Coldwine runner)
2. Run DAG + Blob CAS (CXDB-lite for observability and recovery)
3. Graph Pipeline Orchestration (Coldwine → Attractor-mode)
4. DTU-lite (behavioral API mock generation and conformance testing)
5. Evidence Pack ingestion and replay (Pollard)
6. Agent provenance + capability policies (Bigend tracking, Coldwine enforcement)

MCP integration: autarch-mcp exposes tools for all capabilities. Clavain plugin calls these via MCP protocol. Shared filesystem contract: .clavain/ directory.

{"id":"iv-ho3","title":"Epic: StrongDM Factory Substrate — validation-first infrastructure for Clavain","description":"Autarch becomes the runtime backend for Clavain's StrongDM-inspired factory techniques. Clavain (Claude Code plugin) handles prompt engineering and orchestration; Autarch handles persistent state, background execution, isolation, and observability.

Key capabilities to add:
1. Scenario Bank + Satisfaction Scoring (Gurgeh scenarios + Coldwine runner)
2. Run DAG + Blob CAS (CXDB-lite for observability and recovery)
3. Graph Pipeline Orchestration (Coldwine → Attractor-mode)
4. DTU-lite (behavioral API mock generation and conformance testing)
5. Evidence Pack ingestion and replay (Pollard)
6. Agent provenance + capability policies (Bigend tracking, Coldwine enforcement)

MCP integration: autarch-mcp exposes tools for all capabilities. Clavain plugin calls these via MCP protocol. Shared filesystem contract: .clavain/ directory.

Source: StrongDM Software Factory (factory.strongdm.ai), Oracle GPT-5.2 Pro gap analysis.

{"id":"iv-296","title":"Add CXDB-lite: run DAG + blob CAS for agent observability and recovery","description":"StrongDM's CXDB provides turn DAG, blob dedup, and typed payloads. Adopt the architecture as lightweight embedded infrastructure.

Implementation:
- SQLite tables: turns (id, parent_id, type, payload_hash, timestamp), blobs (hash, content), types (name, schema)
- Filesystem CAS fallback: .clavain/blobs/<blake3-hash> for large artifacts
- Typed event envelopes: autarch.turn.v1, autarch.toolcall.v1, autarch.scenario_result.v1
- O(1) fork: create new turn pointing to existing parent (DAG branching)
- Run manifests: .clavain/runs/<run_id>/manifest.json with pointers into DAG

MCP tools to expose:
- run:start, run:checkpoint, run:resume, run:fork
- run:inspect (returns DAG visualization or summary)
- run:log (append typed event to current run)

This is foundational for Attractor-mode pipelines, scenario recovery, and Bigend observability.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-11T13:05:12Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-02-11T13:33:45Z","work_type":"mutex"}

{"id":"iv-c2r","title":"Add scenario bank + satisfaction scoring to Gurgeh/Coldwine","description":"StrongDM's core validation: end-to-end user stories as holdout sets, scored by satisfaction (not boolean pass/fail).

Gurgeh side:
- New entity type: Scenario (intent, setup, steps, expected observations, rubric, risk tags)
- Store in .clavain/scenarios/dev/ (visible to implementation) and .clavain/scenarios/holdout/ (hidden)
- Scenario versioning alongside spec versioning
- Rubric-based scoring: deterministic checks + LLM-as-judge

Coldwine side:
- Scenario runner: execute scenarios, produce trajectory bundles
- Satisfaction scorer: compute satisfaction metric over trajectories
- Holdout isolation: implementation agents run in worktree WITHOUT .clavain/scenarios/holdout/
- Gate integration: refuse completion unless satisfaction >= threshold on holdouts

MCP tools to expose:
- scenario:create, scenario:list, scenario:run, scenario:score
- satisfaction:check (returns current score + pass/fail for gate)

This is the keystone capability — makes non-interactive execution defensible.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-11T13:05:07Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-02-11T13:33:45Z","work_type":"mutex"}

{"id":"iv-yfkln.1","title":"[intercom] Persist inbound Telegram/WhatsApp messages to Postgres for Rust message loop parity","description":"Ensure inbound channel messages reach the Postgres-backed Rust orchestration path, not just legacy SQLite, so intercomd message loop processes live traffic consistently. Includes Telegram ingress persistence settings, WhatsApp persistence bridge design/implementation, and parity verification.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-27T08:21:26Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-02-27T08:21:26Z"}

{"id":"iv-yfkln.2","title":"[intercom] Unify service install/start flow for intercom + intercomd","description":"Replace split/legacy service wiring with a single supported installer flow that provisions both Node host and Rust daemon with consistent unit names, startup order, and health checks. Eliminate nanoclaw-only service bootstrap assumptions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-27T08:21:26Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-02-27T08:21:26Z"}

{"id":"iv-yfkln.3","title":"[intercom] Migrate legacy config paths and naming from nanoclaw to intercom","description":"Resolve runtime/setup path drift (e.g. ~/.config/nanoclaw vs ~/.config/intercom, service/log/plist naming) with migration-safe compatibility handling and clear deprecation path.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-27T08:21:26Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-02-27T08:21:26Z"}

{"id":"iv-yfkln.4","title":"[intercom] Remove stale runtime toggles/fallbacks after IronClaw cutover","description":"Audit and remove obsolete INTERCOM_ENGINE and cutover-era fallback branches where Rust orchestration is now authoritative, while preserving explicit rollback mechanisms via git/tag strategy.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-27T08:21:26Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-02-27T08:21:26Z"}

{"id":"iv-t712t","title":"P0: First-stranger experience — README, install, clavain setup","description":"No outside user has ever run this. No README getting-started for any pillar. No ic binary distribution. No clavain setup that works for a stranger (iv-frqh is P2). No first-run experience validated by anyone except the author. The learning loop (Interspect) needs diverse data to avoid overfitting to one workflow. The platform play requires strangers succeeding independently. Deliverables: one README, one install script, one clavain setup that works end-to-end for a new user.","notes":"[doc-map] brainstorm (declared): docs/brainstorms/2026-02-23-first-stranger-experience-brainstorm.md","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-23T23:57:31Z","created_by":"mk","updated_at":"2026-02-27T07:31:14Z","work_type":"mutex"}

{"id":"iv-b46xi","title":"P0: Measure north star — cost-per-landable-change baseline","description":"The vision defines a north star metric (cost to ship a reviewed, tested change) but it has never been measured. Even rough numbers change everything — you cannot improve what you do not measure. {"id":"iv-d62c","title":"[intercore] Write-path namespace validation (v1.5) — reject out-of-namespace state writes","description":"First step of the write-path enforcement ladder. ic state set should reject writes outside the caller's declared namespace. Companion plugins declare their namespace at init time. OS-level writes require the os/ prefix. App writes require app/ prefix. Kernel-internal keys require _kernel/ prefix. Implementation: (1) add --caller or IC_CALLER env var support, (2) validate namespace prefix against caller identity, (3) reject mismatched writes with clear error. See intercore-vision.md Write-Path Contract v1.5.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:41:42Z","created_by":"mk","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:41:42Z","work_type":"mutex"}

{"id":"iv-0681","title":"Crash recovery + error aggregation for multi-agent sessions","description":"When sessions crash, interlock Stop hook never fires: orphaned reservations (15min TTL), stale git index files, ghost agents in intermute. Hook errors silently swallowed via || exit 0. No health dashboard or error correlation. Fix: heartbeat-based agent reaper in intermute sweeper, hook health counters, crash detection in SessionStart, error correlation rules, interline escalation. ~3 days. Ref: docs/research/orchestration-gap-analysis.md Gap 1+5, docs/research/user-product-opportunity-review.md #1","notes":"asupersync research (2026-02-19): The crash recovery proposal should implement multi-phase cancellation (iv-cy1s) rather than relying solely on TTL expiry. Region-owned task trees + obligation leak detection (iv-peb6) are complementary patterns from the same source.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-15T12:45:15Z","created_by":"mk","updated_at":"2026-02-20T06:11:26Z","work_type":"mutex"}

{"id":"iv-b46xi","title":"P0: Measure north star — cost-per-landable-change baseline","description":"The vision defines a north star metric (cost to ship a reviewed, tested change) but it has never been measured. After 1748 closed beads, there is no baseline. Instrument token spend, wall-clock time, and agent utilization across 10 sprints to establish a baseline. Even rough numbers change everything — you cannot improve what you do not measure. {"id":"iv-ymm3i","title":"[research] Imbue Darwinian Evolver: evolutionary LLM optimization framework — integration assessment","description":"External sources:
- Paper: https://imbue.com/research/2026-02-27-arc-agi-2-evolution/
- Paper: https://imbue.com/research/2026-02-27-darwinian-evolver/
- Repo: https://github.com/imbue-ai/darwinian_evolver (cloned to docs/research/darwinian_evolver/)
- Inspired by: Darwin Goedel Machines (https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.22954)

Review: docs/research/imbue-darwinian-evolver-arc-agi-2-review-2026-02-27.md

Triage summary:
Imbue open-sources a problem-agnostic evolutionary optimization framework that maintains a population of LLM-generated code/prompt organisms, iteratively selecting, mutating, and evaluating them. Applied to ARC-AGI-2, it triples open-weight model performance (Kimi K2.5: 12.1% → 34.0%) and pushes Gemini 3.1 Pro to 95.1%. Framework is general enough to optimize arbitrary code, prompts, or agents.

Relevance to Demarch:
- Population-level resilience over individual precision — maps to autonomous agent strategy evolution
- Soft scoring for gradual improvement — applicable to agent evaluation
- Learning log for cross-organism knowledge sharing — maps to solution compounding
- Verification filter pattern — cheap pre-check before expensive evaluation
- Crossover mutations — combining strategies from different agents
- Model routing/mixing — already a Clavain concern

Primary Demarch targets: os/clavain, core/intercore, interverse/interflux, apps/autarch
Research topics: evolutionary optimization, LLM-driven code mutation, population dynamics, test-time compute scaling, ARC-AGI-2

Key transferable patterns:
1. Organism/Evaluator/Mutator abstraction — clean 3-component interface for any optimization problem
2. Sigmoid-weighted parent selection with novelty bonus — balances exploit vs explore
3. Learning log with neighborhood strategies — cross-organism knowledge sharing
4. Post-mutation verification filter — reject trivial/unchanged mutations before expensive eval
5. Soft scoring with baseline rescaling — enables gradual fitness improvement
6. Multi-provider LLM mixing — diversity in mutation strategies

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Review document complete (done: docs/research/imbue-darwinian-evolver-arc-agi-2-review-2026-02-27.md)
- [ ] Child beads created for each integration opportunity
- [ ] Each child produces an assessment with adopt/adapt/inspire/avoid verdict","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T02:31:50Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-02-28T02:31:50Z"}

{"id":"iv-g9aju","title":"[research] Assess evolutionary prompt/strategy optimization for Clavain model routing","description":"Investigate whether Imbue's Darwinian Evolver pattern can improve Clavain's model routing decisions.

Current state: Clavain routes tasks to models based on static heuristics and heterogeneous routing config.
Opportunity: Use evolutionary optimization to evolve routing rules based on task outcomes — population of routing strategies, scored by cost-per-quality metrics.

Key questions:
- Can routing rules be expressed as 'organisms' that evolve?
- What would the evaluator look like? (cost, quality, latency tradeoffs)
- Is the population overhead worth it vs. simpler A/B testing?
- How does the learning log pattern map to routing feedback loops?

Reference: docs/research/imbue-darwinian-evolver-arc-agi-2-review-2026-02-27.md §4

{"id":"iv-kt1f4","title":"[research] Assess learning log pattern for Demarch solution compounding","description":"Evaluate whether Imbue's learning log system (attempted_change + observed_outcome tracking with ancestor/neighborhood sharing strategies) can enhance Demarch's existing solution compounding workflow.

Current state: Clavain /compound captures solved problems as docs. No structured cross-session feedback on what mutations/fixes worked vs failed.
Opportunity: Learning log with neighborhood-N strategy enables agents to learn from nearby failed attempts, not just successes.

Key questions:
- How does learning log compare to existing MEMORY.md + docs/solutions pattern?
- Could neighborhood-based sharing improve multi-agent coordination?
- What's the storage/retrieval overhead?
- Does the ancestor vs neighborhood strategy matter for agent workflows?

{"id":"iv-roymw","title":"[research] Assess verification filter pattern for Interflux review agents","description":"Evaluate whether Imbue's post-mutation verification filter can reduce wasted computation in Interflux multi-agent review workflows.

Current state: Interflux dispatches multiple reviewer agents (architecture, correctness, quality, etc.) in parallel. Each runs full analysis regardless of whether changes are substantive.
Opportunity: Cheap pre-check (like Imbue's 'did the output actually change?') before launching expensive full reviews.

Key questions:
- Can we cheaply classify whether a code change is substantive before full review?
- What's the false-negative risk of filtering? (missing real issues)
- How does this interact with flux-drive's agent triage?
- Is the cost savings meaningful given current review costs?

{"id":"iv-wvaq7","title":"[research] Assess evolutionary agent strategy optimization for Autarch","description":"Evaluate whether Imbue's evolutionary framework can be applied to evolving autonomous agent strategies in Autarch.

Current state: Autarch agents use fixed strategies/prompts. No mechanism for systematic strategy improvement based on task outcomes.
Opportunity: Maintain a population of agent configurations (prompts, tool preferences, reasoning approaches), evolve them based on task success metrics.

Key questions:
- What constitutes an 'organism' for agent strategies? (prompt? tool selection? full config?)
- What would soft scoring look like for agent performance? (partial task completion?)
- Is crossover meaningful? (combining strategies from agents that excel at different task types)
- How does population management interact with multi-agent orchestration?
- Cost: is evolutionary overhead justified for agent improvement?

new_value:
{"description":"Quality gate findings from H2 last mile sprint (iv-x6gd2) deferred for follow-up:

1. **IpcDelegate decoupling** (P0 architecture): IpcDelegate trait references Telegram-specific InlineKeyboardMarkup type. Extract to an opaque ButtonMarkup type in intercom-core, convert at Telegram boundary.

2. **Callback endpoint auth** (P0 safety): /v1/telegram/callback (and other intercomd endpoints) have no authentication. Consistent with existing security model (localhost-only binding), but should add shared-secret or Unix socket binding for defense in depth.

3. **Stub WriteOperation variants**: Add RejectGate, DeferGate, ExtendBudget, CancelRun to intercom-core WriteOperation enum. Wire to ic CLI commands. Re-enable Reject/Defer buttons for gate notifications and Extend/Cancel for budget notifications.

4. **Budget action buttons**: Currently removed from budget.exceeded notifications. Re-add once ExtendBudget/CancelRun variants are implemented."}

{"id":"iv-vkb9z","title":"[intercore] ic publish init: automate full first-install pipeline","description":"ic publish init currently only adds the plugin to marketplace.json and prints manual instructions. It should automate the full chain: (1) commit + push marketplace repo, (2) RebuildCache (clone into ~/.claude/plugins/cache/), (3) UpdateInstalled (update installed_plugins.json), (4) add settings entry (name@marketplace: true in ~/.claude/settings.json), (5) SyncCCMarketplace. All these steps are already implemented in engine.Publish() Phase 7 — init just needs to call them too. File: core/intercore/cmd/ic/publish.go cmdPublishInit() and core/intercore/internal/publish/engine.go. Currently the gap causes every new plugin creation to require 3 manual steps (cache populate, settings edit, session restart).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T17:25:21Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-02-28T17:25:21Z"}

{"description":"Post-feature integration tracer. Given a recently shipped feature and the data/events it produces, systematically walks the monorepo module graph to find consumers that should read this data but don't. Produces ranked beads for each integration gap. The technique was validated manually during the iv-5muhg disagreement pipeline sprint — found 5 gaps across 4 modules (intersynth, interspect, clavain/galiana, interwatch), all shipped same session. Goal: automate this as a Clavain companion plugin with a slash command (/intertrace) and optional interflux agent (fd-integration)."}

{"id":"iv-db5pc","title":"[fd] Implement Safety Floors for Safety-Critical Agents","description":"From flux-drive research on new routing architecture:

Recent heterogeneous routing experiments revealed a critical quality risk: safety-critical agents are frequently running on weaker, cheaper models (e.g., fd-safety on Haiku 47% of the time, fd-correctness 26% of the time).

Action: Implement mandatory 'safety floors' (e.g., min_model: sonnet or min_model: opus) in the routing configuration to ensure token-cost optimization does not degrade security and code correctness.

Clavain's model routing currently relies on three independent, static systems: Dispatch tiers for Codex, Agent frontmatter for Claude subagents, and Interspect overrides.

{"id":"iv-d1mcu","title":"intermem: automatic structural tidy for memory files","description":"Extend intermem's sweep to do structural housekeeping — not just entry-level decay, but file-level reorganization. Should be automatic (SessionStart or periodic) and token-efficient (no LLM calls for mechanical operations).

Three capabilities:
1. Line budget enforcement — when MEMORY.md exceeds ~120 lines, automatically extract topic sections into linked topic files (deterministic, no LLM needed)
2. Stale count pruning — detect point-in-time counts/snapshots (e.g. '47 commands', '15 skills') that are guaranteed stale and flag or remove them
3. Historical collapse — when an entry has 'before the fix' context that's now baked into code, collapse to just the gotcha

Token efficiency constraints:
- Structural operations (line counting, section extraction, file splitting) should be pure shell/Python — no LLM calls
- Only use LLM for semantic judgment calls (is this entry still relevant?) and keep those to haiku-tier
- The sweep should complete in <5 seconds for a typical memory directory

{"description":"phaseCostEstimate() in budget.go and _sprint_phase_cost_estimate() in lib-sprint.sh use hardcoded constants from Feb 2026 that never update from real data. This is the first concrete instance of the closed-loop-by-default principle (PHILOSOPHY.md) — phase cost estimation has stages 1-2 (defaults + collect actuals) but not stages 3-4 (calibrate + fallback).

Implement:
1. calibrate-phase-costs CLI command in budget.go — queries interstat by-phase history, computes weighted averages per phase, writes to a config file (.clavain/phase-cost-calibration.json)
2. Update phaseCostEstimate() to read calibration file first, fall back to hardcoded defaults
3. Mirror in lib-sprint.sh _sprint_phase_cost_estimate() — same config file, same fallback
4. Add calibration trigger in reflect.md — after capturing learnings, run calibrate-phase-costs silently
5. Tests: verify calibration file is read, verify fallback to defaults when no file exists

The fleet system (estimate-costs.sh + fleet-registry.yaml) is the existence proof. This applies the same 4-stage pattern to sprint phase estimates. Other domains (complexity scoring, gate thresholds, review triage) should follow the same pattern — tracked as separate beads."}

old_value:
{"id":"iv-zzr4i","title":"[tldr-swinton] tldrs CLI crashes with ModuleNotFoundError: pydantic in fresh uv tool install","description":"Repro (observed on 2026-03-02):
1) Install/use tldr-swinton via uv tool (`tldrs` shim points to `~/.local/share/uv/tools/tldr-swinton`)
2) Run:
   `cd /home/mk/projects/Typhon && tldrs diff-context --project . --budget 2000 --max-lines 80`
3) Crash:
   ```
   Traceback (most recent call last):
     File "/home/mk/.local/bin/tldrs", line 4, in <module>
       from tldr_swinton.cli import main
     ...
     File ".../tldr_swinton/modules/core/mcp_server.py", line 21, in <module>
       from pydantic import Field
   ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'pydantic'
   ```

Likely cause:
- `src/tldr_swinton/__init__.py` imports `modules.core.mcp_server` eagerly.
- `mcp_server` imports `pydantic` unconditionally.
- `pyproject.toml` does not declare `pydantic` in base dependencies.

Impact:
- Core CLI commands (e.g. `diff-context`) fail at import time even when MCP mode is not used.

Suggested fixes:
- Preferred: make `mcp_server` import lazy/optional so normal CLI path does not require `pydantic`.
- Also acceptable: add `pydantic` to required base dependencies if always needed.
- Add a regression test that executes `tldrs diff-context` in a minimal env without optional extras.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T16:54:06Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-03-02T16:54:06Z"}

{"id":"iv-nrjbp","title":"[intercom] Fix Hermes-discovered scheduler and queue reliability bugs","description":"7 P1 bugs discovered via flux-drive research analysis of Hermes Agent gateway patterns against intercom's Rust codebase. These are real reliability risks in the intercomd scheduler and queue:\\n\\n1. Scheduler crash window: no atomic task claim (iv-lzyfp)\\n2. Log+update non-atomic: two independent SQL calls (iv-ef2up)\\n3. PgPool stale client: no eviction after connection loss (iv-22kzq)\\n4. IPC filename collision: rand_u16 only 16-bit entropy (iv-zhx8a)\\n5. DB operation timeouts missing: group starvation risk (iv-zmsaa)\\n6. Path traversal: no group_folder validation in Rust queue (iv-tl5jj)\\n7. Cron/timezone validation deferred to execution time (iv-34pq2)\\n\\nTotal estimated effort: ~8.5 days. Blocker for safe scheduler operation.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T20:16:36Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-03-02T20:16:36Z"}

old_value: {"id":"iv-nt43u","title":"Phase 1: Postgres outbox table + LISTEN/NOTIFY","description":"Replace fire-and-forget HTTP dual-writes and 1s polling with durable outbox pattern and event-driven wakeup. Steps: (1) Create outbox table in ensure_schema() with columns: id, chat_jid, payload_type, payload JSONB, status TEXT default 'pending', created_at, delivered_at. (2) Node writes to outbox instead of HTTP POST — INSERT in same transaction as SQLite write. (3) Add NOTIFY trigger on outbox INSERT. (4) Rust LISTEN loop with dedicated tokio-postgres connection, reconnect with exponential backoff. (5) Drain outbox: SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED, mark delivered after processing. (6) 30s fallback poll catches missed NOTIFYs. Effort: ~6 days.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:03:51Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-03-03T00:03:51Z"}

{"id":"iv-nt43u","title":"Phase 1: Postgres outbox table + LISTEN/NOTIFY","description":"Replace fire-and-forget HTTP dual-writes and 1s polling with durable outbox pattern and event-driven wakeup. Steps: (1) Create outbox table in ensure_schema() with columns: id, chat_jid, payload_type, payload JSONB, status TEXT default 'pending', created_at, delivered_at. (2) Node writes to outbox instead of HTTP POST — INSERT in same transaction as SQLite write. (3) Add NOTIFY trigger on outbox INSERT. (4) Rust LISTEN loop with dedicated tokio-postgres connection, reconnect with exponential backoff. (5) Drain outbox: SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED, mark delivered after processing. (6) 30s fallback poll catches missed NOTIFYs. Effort: ~6 days.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:03:51Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-03-03T00:03:51Z"}

{"id":"iv-rttr5","title":"Instrument agent sessions for tool selection failure classification","description":"Measure where agent tool selection actually fails during real sessions. For each failure, classify as: (1) discovery failure — agent didn't surface the right tool candidate, (2) sequencing failure — found right tools but wrong call order or missed preconditions, (3) scale degradation — inherent accuracy loss at 50+ tools regardless of composition. This is the empirical foundation for all composition layer decisions. Output from the plugin-modularity dialectic (3 rounds, 18 artifacts in dialectics/2026-03-02-plugin-modularity/). Source: hostile auditor's closing verdict — 'the composition paradox is an empirical question masquerading as a philosophical one.'","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T18:20:51Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-03-03T18:20:51Z"}

Source: hostile auditor's closing verdict — 'the composition paradox is an empirical question masquerading as a philosophical one.'","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T18:20:51Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-03-03T18:20:51Z"}

{"id":"iv-3kpfu","title":"Build shallow composition layer using interchart architecture","description":"Apply interchart's existing domain overlay architecture (pattern rules + forced curation groups) to agent tool routing. Create tool-routing metadata: (1) tags and domain groupings for discovery, (2) co-occurrence signals from successful sessions as auto-generated routing, (3) one-line sequencing hints where data shows sequencing failures (e.g. 'typically called after interpath.resolve'). Use interchart's OVERLAP_DOMAIN_RULES + FORCED_OVERLAP_GROUPS pattern. The composition layer should be learned from telemetry, not comprehensively hand-authored. Output from plugin-modularity dialectic Round 2-3. Depends on Step 1 instrumentation data to know where to add hints.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T18:20:56Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-03-03T18:20:56Z"}

{"id":"iv-u74sq","title":"Measure accuracy gap with and without composition layer","description":"Compare agent tool selection accuracy with and without the shallow composition layer on identical tasks. The 18-point gap (Tool Search 74% vs native 5-7 tools 92%) should decompose into: (1) portion closed by shallow metadata — 'just infrastructure' works, (2) portion requiring sequencing knowledge — needs moderate composition hints, (3) portion requiring deep docs — for these plugin pairs consolidation is the honest response, (4) irreducible scale portion — wait for model improvements (Opus 4.5 already at 88.1%). This measurement determines whether the moderate composition band is real or a 'polite fiction' (Round 3 auditor). Output from plugin-modularity dialectic Round 3.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T18:20:58Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-03-03T18:20:58Z"}

{"id":"iv-mtf12","title":"Let data determine plugin boundary decisions","description":"For each plugin pair with high sequencing failure rates (from Step 3 measurement): (1) if one-line hints close it — keep separate, add hint to tool description, (2) if hints don't close it and deep docs are needed — consolidate or introduce facade, (3) if model improvements close it — do nothing, problem is temporary. This replaces all philosophical mechanisms (stranger test, tier tables, commit history) with empirical measurement. Key constraint: do NOT reclassify 49 plugins into tiers, do NOT statically bundle by workflow, do NOT hand-author comprehensive composition docs. Let instrumentation data drive boundary decisions. Output from plugin-modularity dialectic 3-round conclusion.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T18:21:01Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-03-03T18:21:01Z"}

old_value:
{"id":"iv-gs4vo","title":"Plugin: /ui-analyze — automated UI/UX design analysis from any URL","description":"Build a Claude Code plugin that deeply analyzes websites for design inspiration. Accepts a URL and emits a structured design system report (W3C DTCG tokens, component inventory, UX state atlas).

## Why
No existing plugin/skill systematically extracts design systems from live websites. This is a recurring need across projects (Shadow Work, future projects). The tooling landscape is mature enough to build this now — Playwright MCP covers 95%+ of browser automation needs, Dembrandt handles token extraction, and Claude vision fills the gap for layout/aesthetic analysis.

## What
A plugin with a /ui-analyze skill that orchestrates:
1. Dembrandt CLI for baseline token extraction (colors, typography, spacing → W3C DTCG JSON)
2. Playwright MCP browser_evaluate for deep CSS variable harvesting, computed style analysis, font loading
3. Playwright MCP browser_snapshot (ARIA tree) for component inventory and semantic structure
4. Playwright MCP browser_take_screenshot + Claude vision for layout patterns, chart analysis, aesthetic judgment
5. Network interception via page.route() for non-happy-path state discovery (error, empty, loading, onboarding)
6. intercache MCP for per-URL caching with content-hash invalidation

## Output artifacts
- W3C Design Tokens JSON (DTCG format) — colors, typography, spacing, elevation, borders
- Component inventory (Markdown + JSON) — DOM signatures, interaction states, screenshots
- UX state atlas (YAML) — screen → state mapping with screenshots and DOM fingerprints
- Layout analysis (Markdown) — grid systems, breakpoints, information hierarchy

## Depth modes
- tokens (~$0.02/URL): CSS variables + computed styles only, no screenshots
- standard (~$0.04/URL): + 1 screenshot for layout analysis
- full (~$0.07/URL): + multi-breakpoint screenshots, hover states, state atlas

## Effort estimate
10-14 hours: 2h orchestrator, 4-6h JS extraction scripts (~365 lines across 9 files), 1-2h output schema, testing

## Research
Full 5-agent research synthesis at shadow-work/.clavain/scratch/synthesis-uiux-design-analysis.md
Individual agent findings at shadow-work/.clavain/scratch/fd-playwright-dom-extraction.md, fd-css-design-token-reverse-engineering.md, fd-component-pattern-cataloging.md, fd-ux-flow-and-state-analysis.md, fd-uiux-workflow-plugin.md","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T19:23:23Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-03-03T19:23:23Z"}

{"id":"iv-jntjbo","title":"P0-A: Retry deferred flag never drained — groups stuck after failure","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P0
- labels: bug
- estimate: 120
- description: queue.rs retry path spawns async task that sets pending_messages=true after delay, but drain_pending has already run. Nothing re-triggers draining — group stuck until new external message arrives. Node equivalent correctly calls enqueueMessageCheck in timer callback. Fix: retry task must call drain_pending or enqueue_message_check after setting the flag, not just set the flag. Refs: queue.rs:424-441
- metadata: {"mod":"intercom","agent":"fd-queue-delivery-reliability,fd-container-lifecycle-management","file":"rust/intercomd/src/queue.rs"}","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z"}

{"id":"iv-oa4muh","title":"P0-B: Registered groups never propagate from Node to Postgres","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P0
- labels: bug
- estimate: 240
- description: register_group IPC writes to SQLite only (db.ts:686 setRegisteredGroup). No dualWriteToPostgres, no writeToOutbox, no HTTP POST to /v1/db/groups/set. Rust orchestrator loads groups from Postgres at startup (main.rs:253-277) and caches in memory. Newly registered groups are invisible to orchestrator — messages silently skipped at process_group.rs:100-103. Fix: add outbox entry for group registration or call intercomd /v1/db/groups/set from the IPC handler. Refs: src/db.ts:686, rust/intercomd/src/process_group.rs:100-103
- metadata: {"mod":"intercom","agent":"fd-dual-database-state-divergence,fd-ipc-authorization-boundary","file":"src/db.ts,rust/intercomd/src/process_group.rs"}","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z"}

{"id":"iv-9j2tb2","title":"P1-A: drain_pending dispatches tasks to message path (infinite loop)","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P1
- labels: bug
- estimate: 120
- description: drain_pending finds groups with pending_tasks, marks them active, but always spawns run_for_group (message path). Tasks are never popped from pending_tasks or passed to run_task. Creates infinite loop — run_for_group finds no messages, succeeds, triggers drain_pending, sees same pending tasks, repeats. Node drainGroup correctly pops and runs tasks. Fix: check pending_tasks in drain_pending, pop and route to run_task. Refs: queue.rs:459-503
- metadata: {"mod":"intercom","agent":"fd-queue-delivery-reliability,fd-container-lifecycle-management","file":"rust/intercomd/src/queue.rs"}","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z"}

{"id":"iv-m9qpoz","title":"P1-B: register_process never called — container lifecycle broken","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P1
- labels: bug
- estimate: 90
- description: queue.rs:236 register_process is defined but has zero callers. container_name and group_folder are always None on every GroupState. Breaks kill_group, send_message, close_stdin, notify_idle (task preemption), and shutdown container reporting. Every IPC-based feature of the queue is silently non-functional in Rust orchestrator. Fix: call register_process from run_for_group/run_task after container spawn succeeds. Refs: queue.rs:236
- metadata: {"mod":"intercom","agent":"fd-container-lifecycle-management","file":"rust/intercomd/src/queue.rs"}","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z"}

{"id":"iv-lw0kho","title":"P1-C: Outbox zombie rows stuck after 5 retries","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P1
- labels: bug
- estimate: 60
- description: After 5 transient failures, mark_outbox_retry resets row to status=pending but attempts stays at 5. claim_outbox_rows requires attempts < 5, so row is permanently invisible — never claimed, never marked failed. outbox_stats misleadingly reports as pending. Fix: either increment max attempts threshold in claim query, or mark as failed after max retries instead of resetting to pending. Refs: persistence.rs:1241
- metadata: {"mod":"intercom","agent":"fd-queue-delivery-reliability","file":"rust/intercomd/src/persistence.rs"}","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z"}

{"id":"iv-h2z56u","title":"P1-D: Mount exclude values unvalidated — path traversal possible","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P1
- labels: bug,security
- estimate: 60
- description: exclude array from AdditionalMount interpolated directly into --mount type=tmpfs,destination= arguments without checking for .., /, or comma. Allows path traversal inside container or Docker mount option injection via comma-separated values. Both Node (container-runner.ts:291-293) and Rust (secrets.rs:153-159) share this gap. Fix: validate exclude values — reject entries containing .., leading /, or commas. Refs: container-runner.ts:291-293, secrets.rs:153-159
- metadata: {"mod":"intercom","agent":"fd-mount-security-wiring","file":"src/container-runner.ts,rust/intercomd/src/container/secrets.rs"}","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z"}

{"id":"iv-yy4yw3","title":"P1-E: No panic guard on run_for_group — permanent stuck on panic","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P1
- labels: bug
- estimate: 60
- description: If process_messages_fn panics, tokio task dies without reaching reset_group. Group permanently stuck with active=true, blocking all future work and leaking a concurrency slot. Node uses try/finally which always runs cleanup. Fix: wrap run_for_group body in catch_unwind or use a Drop guard that calls reset_group. Refs: queue.rs:386
- metadata: {"mod":"intercom","agent":"fd-container-lifecycle-management","file":"rust/intercomd/src/queue.rs"}","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z"}

{"id":"iv-gr1n8n","title":"P1-F: Dual IPC polling race — non-deterministic authorization","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P1
- labels: bug,security
- estimate: 180
- description: Both Node and Rust poll data/ipc/ on 1-second intervals with different authorization logic. Node uses always-fresh in-memory state; Rust uses GroupRegistry up to 10s stale. For tasks, Node applies full authorization while Rust applies none. Which process picks up a file depends on filesystem race. Fix: designate one process as IPC authority (Rust when orchestrator enabled) or use file locking. Refs: src/ipc.ts:45-198, rust/intercomd/src/ipc.rs:234-258
- metadata: {"mod":"intercom","agent":"fd-ipc-authorization-boundary","file":"src/ipc.ts,rust/intercomd/src/ipc.rs"}","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z"}

{"id":"iv-jntjbo","title":"P0-A: Retry deferred flag never drained — groups stuck after failure","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P0
- labels: bug
- estimate: 120
- description: queue.rs retry path spawns async task that sets pending_messages=true after delay, but drain_pending has already run. Nothing re-triggers draining — group stuck until new external message arrives. Node equivalent correctly calls enqueueMessageCheck in timer callback. Fix: retry task must call drain_pending or enqueue_message_check after setting the flag, not just set the flag. Refs: queue.rs:424-441
- metadata: {"mod":"intercom","agent":"fd-queue-delivery-reliability,fd-container-lifecycle-management","file":"rust/intercomd/src/queue.rs"}","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z"}

{"id":"iv-oa4muh","title":"P0-B: Registered groups never propagate from Node to Postgres","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P0
- labels: bug
- estimate: 240
- description: register_group IPC writes to SQLite only (db.ts:686 setRegisteredGroup). No dualWriteToPostgres, no writeToOutbox, no HTTP POST to /v1/db/groups/set. Rust orchestrator loads groups from Postgres at startup (main.rs:253-277) and caches in memory. Newly registered groups are invisible to orchestrator — messages silently skipped at process_group.rs:100-103. Fix: add outbox entry for group registration or call intercomd /v1/db/groups/set from the IPC handler. Refs: src/db.ts:686, rust/intercomd/src/process_group.rs:100-103
- metadata: {"mod":"intercom","agent":"fd-dual-database-state-divergence,fd-ipc-authorization-boundary","file":"src/db.ts,rust/intercomd/src/process_group.rs"}","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z"}

{"id":"iv-9j2tb2","title":"P1-A: drain_pending dispatches tasks to message path (infinite loop)","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P1
- labels: bug
- estimate: 120
- description: drain_pending finds groups with pending_tasks, marks them active, but always spawns run_for_group (message path). Tasks are never popped from pending_tasks or passed to run_task. Creates infinite loop — run_for_group finds no messages, succeeds, triggers drain_pending, sees same pending tasks, repeats. Node drainGroup correctly pops and runs tasks. Fix: check pending_tasks in drain_pending, pop and route to run_task. Refs: queue.rs:459-503
- metadata: {"mod":"intercom","agent":"fd-queue-delivery-reliability,fd-container-lifecycle-management","file":"rust/intercomd/src/queue.rs"}","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z"}

{"id":"iv-m9qpoz","title":"P1-B: register_process never called — container lifecycle broken","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P1
- labels: bug
- estimate: 90
- description: queue.rs:236 register_process is defined but has zero callers. container_name and group_folder are always None on every GroupState. Breaks kill_group, send_message, close_stdin, notify_idle (task preemption), and shutdown container reporting. Every IPC-based feature of the queue is silently non-functional in Rust orchestrator. Fix: call register_process from run_for_group/run_task after container spawn succeeds. Refs: queue.rs:236
- metadata: {"mod":"intercom","agent":"fd-container-lifecycle-management","file":"rust/intercomd/src/queue.rs"}","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z"}

{"id":"iv-lw0kho","title":"P1-C: Outbox zombie rows stuck after 5 retries","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P1
- labels: bug
- estimate: 60
- description: After 5 transient failures, mark_outbox_retry resets row to status=pending but attempts stays at 5. claim_outbox_rows requires attempts < 5, so row is permanently invisible — never claimed, never marked failed. outbox_stats misleadingly reports as pending. Fix: either increment max attempts threshold in claim query, or mark as failed after max retries instead of resetting to pending. Refs: persistence.rs:1241
- metadata: {"mod":"intercom","agent":"fd-queue-delivery-reliability","file":"rust/intercomd/src/persistence.rs"}","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z"}

{"id":"iv-h2z56u","title":"P1-D: Mount exclude values unvalidated — path traversal possible","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P1
- labels: bug,security
- estimate: 60
- description: exclude array from AdditionalMount interpolated directly into --mount type=tmpfs,destination= arguments without checking for .., /, or comma. Allows path traversal inside container or Docker mount option injection via comma-separated values. Both Node (container-runner.ts:291-293) and Rust (secrets.rs:153-159) share this gap. Fix: validate exclude values — reject entries containing .., leading /, or commas. Refs: container-runner.ts:291-293, secrets.rs:153-159
- metadata: {"mod":"intercom","agent":"fd-mount-security-wiring","file":"src/container-runner.ts,rust/intercomd/src/container/secrets.rs"}","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z"}

{"id":"iv-45jlmn","title":"P1-G: No Rust task authorization — tasks forwarded blindly","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P1
- labels: bug,security
- estimate: 90
- description: Rust IPC process_tasks() forwards all tasks to Node without any authorization check (ipc.rs:308-329). IpcDelegate forward_task signature does not enforce authorization. If Rust takes over native task handling, no authorization layer exists. Fix: replicate Node-side task authorization checks (sourceGroup validation, is_main check for register_group) in Rust before forwarding. Refs: rust/intercomd/src/ipc.rs:308-329
- metadata: {"mod":"intercom","agent":"fd-ipc-authorization-boundary","file":"rust/intercomd/src/ipc.rs"}","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z"}

{"id":"iv-epv9tb","title":"P1-H: Rust GroupRegistry 10s stale window — new groups silently denied","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P1
- labels: bug
- estimate: 60
- description: GroupRegistry syncs from Node every 10 seconds (ipc.rs:694-750). During startup (first 12s) and between sync cycles, newly registered groups are invisible to Rust message authorization. Legitimate messages from new groups silently denied and deleted (not moved to errors). Fix: sync on demand when an unknown group is seen, or use push-based notification from Node on registration. Refs: rust/intercomd/src/ipc.rs:694-750
- metadata: {"mod":"intercom","agent":"fd-ipc-authorization-boundary","file":"rust/intercomd/src/ipc.rs"}","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z"}

{"id":"iv-fjkrqx","title":"P2-A: WhatsApp /model command writes SQLite only — Rust spawns stale model","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P2
- labels: bug
- estimate: 60
- description: WhatsApp /model command in src/index.ts:260-266 updates model/runtime in SQLite without propagating to Postgres. Rust orchestrator spawns containers with stale model. Telegram commands correctly go through Rust /v1/commands endpoint. Fix: add dualWriteToPostgres call or route through intercomd. Refs: src/index.ts:260-266
- metadata: {"mod":"intercom","agent":"fd-dual-database-state-divergence","file":"src/index.ts"}","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z"}

{"id":"iv-acikxi","title":"P2-B: Tasks created via Node IPC are SQLite-only — Rust scheduler never sees them","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P2
- labels: bug
- estimate: 90
- description: createTask() in ipc.ts:303 writes to SQLite. Rust scheduler reads from Postgres via pool.claim_due_tasks() and never finds these tasks. Task mutations (pause/resume/cancel) in ipc.ts:322-374 also SQLite-only. Rust continues executing paused/deleted tasks. Fix: propagate task writes to Postgres via outbox or direct HTTP. Refs: src/ipc.ts:303, src/ipc.ts:322-374
- metadata: {"mod":"intercom","agent":"fd-dual-database-state-divergence","file":"src/ipc.ts"}","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z"}

{"id":"iv-0mz6yr","title":"P2-C: Outbox supports only 2 payload types — blocks propagation fixes","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P2
- labels: bug
- estimate: 120
- description: writeToOutbox accepts only message and chat_metadata. Rust drain hard-matches on these, marking all other types as permanent failures. This blocks using outbox pattern to fix entity propagation gaps (groups, tasks, sessions, model). Fix: extend outbox schema to support group_registration, task, session payload types. Refs: src/pg-writer.ts, rust/intercomd/src/outbox.rs

{"id":"iv-lw0kho","title":"P1-C: Outbox zombie rows stuck after 5 retries","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P1
- labels: bug
- estimate: 60
- description: After 5 transient failures, mark_outbox_retry resets row to status=pending but attempts stays at 5. claim_outbox_rows requires attempts < 5, so row is permanently invisible — never claimed, never marked failed. outbox_stats misleadingly reports as pending. Fix: either increment max attempts threshold in claim query, or mark as failed after max retries instead of resetting to pending. Refs: persistence.rs:1241
- metadata: {"mod":"intercom","agent":"fd-queue-delivery-reliability","file":"rust/intercomd/src/persistence.rs"}","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:58Z"}

{"id":"iv-h2z56u","title":"P1-D: Mount exclude values unvalidated — path traversal possible","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P1
- labels: bug,security
- estimate: 60
- description: exclude array from AdditionalMount interpolated directly into --mount type=tmpfs,destination= arguments without checking for .., /, or comma. Allows path traversal inside container or Docker mount option injection via comma-separated values. Both Node (container-runner.ts:291-293) and Rust (secrets.rs:153-159) share this gap. Fix: validate exclude values — reject entries containing .., leading /, or commas. Refs: container-runner.ts:291-293, secrets.rs:153-159

{"id":"iv-yy4yw3","title":"P1-E: No panic guard on run_for_group — permanent stuck on panic","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P1
- labels: bug
- estimate: 60
- description: If process_messages_fn panics, tokio task dies without reaching reset_group. Group permanently stuck with active=true, blocking all future work and leaking a concurrency slot. Node uses try/finally which always runs cleanup. Fix: wrap run_for_group body in catch_unwind or use a Drop guard that calls reset_group. Refs: queue.rs:386
- metadata: {"mod":"intercom","agent":"fd-container-lifecycle-management","file":"rust/intercomd/src/queue.rs"}","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:58Z"}

{"id":"iv-oa4muh","title":"P0-B: Registered groups never propagate from Node to Postgres","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P0
- labels: bug
- estimate: 240
- description: register_group IPC writes to SQLite only (db.ts:686 setRegisteredGroup). No dualWriteToPostgres, no writeToOutbox, no HTTP POST to /v1/db/groups/set. Rust orchestrator loads groups from Postgres at startup (main.rs:253-277) and caches in memory. Newly registered groups are invisible to orchestrator — messages silently skipped at process_group.rs:100-103. Fix: add outbox entry for group registration or call intercomd /v1/db/groups/set from the IPC handler. Refs: src/db.ts:686, rust/intercomd/src/process_group.rs:100-103
- metadata: {"mod":"intercom","agent":"fd-dual-database-state-divergence,fd-ipc-authorization-boundary","file":"src/db.ts,rust/intercomd/src/process_group.rs"}","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:51Z","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:21:57Z"}

{"id":"iv-m9qpoz","title":"P1-B: register_process never called — container lifecycle broken","description":"- type: bug
- priority: P1
- labels: bug
- estimate: 90
- description: queue.rs:236 register_process is defined but has zero callers. container_name and group_folder are always None on every GroupState. Breaks kill_group, send_message, close_stdin, notify_idle (task preemption), and shutdown container reporting. Every IPC-based feature of the queue is silently non-functional in Rust orchestrator. Fix: call register_process from run_for_group/run_task after container spawn succeeds. Refs: queue.rs:236

Added on_spawn callback to run_container_agent. Both process_group.rs and scheduler_wiring.rs now call queue.register_process() immediately after container spawn, enabling kill_group, send_message, and notify_idle to find the container.

Added groups_fallback to GroupRegistry. folder_for_jid now checks Rust in-memory groups map (updated immediately by set-sync) when periodic sync map misses. Eliminates 10s stale window for newly registered groups.

When INTERCOMD_URL is set, Node IPC watcher exits early — Rust daemon is sole IPC authority. Eliminates dual-polling race. Rust forwards messages/tasks to Node via HTTP delegate.

Extended outbox with group_registration and task payload types. Rust drain now handles both: groups are stored in Postgres and in-memory map, tasks are created in Postgres. Node writeToOutbox type union updated.

old_value: {"id":"iv-fkq60","title":"Phase 2: Container UDS IPC replaces stdout markers","description":"Replace ---INTERCOM_OUTPUT_START/END--- stdout marker parsing with Unix domain socket IPC between runner and container. Steps: (1) Mount tmpfs at /tmp/agent-ipc/ shared between host and container. (2) Container creates UDS server, host connects after spawn. (3) Framed protocol: 4-byte length prefix + JSON payload. (4) Keep stdout markers as degraded fallback for containers that don't support UDS. (5) Update extract_output_markers to prefer UDS when available. Eliminates partial-read, truncation, and marker-boundary bugs. Effort: ~3 days.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:03:55Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-03-03T00:03:55Z"}

{"id":"iv-fkq60","title":"Phase 2: Container UDS IPC replaces stdout markers","description":"Replace ---INTERCOM_OUTPUT_START/END--- stdout marker parsing with Unix domain socket IPC between runner and container. Steps: (1) Mount tmpfs at /tmp/agent-ipc/ shared between host and container. (2) Container creates UDS server, host connects after spawn. (3) Framed protocol: 4-byte length prefix + JSON payload. (4) Keep stdout markers as degraded fallback for containers that don't support UDS. (5) Update extract_output_markers to prefer UDS when available. Eliminates partial-read, truncation, and marker-boundary bugs. Effort: ~3 days.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"mistakeknot","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:03:55Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-03-05T00:18:45Z"}

{"id":"iv-296","title":"Add CXDB-lite: run DAG + blob CAS for agent observability and recovery","description":"StrongDM's CXDB provides turn DAG, blob dedup, and typed payloads. Adopt the architecture as lightweight embedded infrastructure.

Implementation:
- SQLite tables: turns (id, parent_id, type, payload_hash, timestamp), blobs (hash, content), types (name, schema)
- Filesystem CAS fallback: .clavain/blobs/<blake3-hash> for large artifacts
- Typed event envelopes: autarch.turn.v1, autarch.toolcall.v1, autarch.scenario_result.v1
- O(1) fork: create new turn pointing to existing parent (DAG branching)
- Run manifests: .clavain/runs/<run_id>/manifest.json with pointers into DAG

MCP tools to expose:
- run:start, run:checkpoint, run:resume, run:fork
- run:inspect (returns DAG visualization or summary)
- run:log (append typed event to current run)

This is foundational for Attractor-mode pipelines, scenario recovery, and Bigend observability.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-11T13:05:12Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-02-27T08:10:29Z","work_type":"mutex"}

{"id":"iv-c2r","title":"Add scenario bank + satisfaction scoring to Gurgeh/Coldwine","description":"StrongDM's core validation: end-to-end user stories as holdout sets, scored by satisfaction (not boolean pass/fail).

Gurgeh side:
- New entity type: Scenario (intent, setup, steps, expected observations, rubric, risk tags)
- Store in .clavain/scenarios/dev/ (visible to implementation) and .clavain/scenarios/holdout/ (hidden)
- Scenario versioning alongside spec versioning
- Rubric-based scoring: deterministic checks + LLM-as-judge

Coldwine side:
- Scenario runner: execute scenarios, produce trajectory bundles
- Satisfaction scorer: compute satisfaction metric over trajectories
- Holdout isolation: implementation agents run in worktree WITHOUT .clavain/scenarios/holdout/
- Gate integration: refuse completion unless satisfaction >= threshold on holdouts

MCP tools to expose:
- scenario:create, scenario:list, scenario:run, scenario:score
- satisfaction:check (returns current score + pass/fail for gate)

This is the keystone capability — makes non-interactive execution defensible.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-11T13:05:07Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-02-27T08:10:29Z","work_type":"mutex"}

{"id":"iv-3ov","title":"Add evidence pack pipeline to Pollard for obstacle-to-input conversion","description":"StrongDM's Apply More Tokens: convert every obstacle into model-consumable evidence.

Implementation:
- Evidence pack structure: .clavain/evidence/<case_id>/ with logs/, screens/, traces/, replays/, customer-notes/
- manifest.yml: provenance, format, replay instructions
- Ingestion: normalize logs, screenshots, conversation transcripts into structured format
- Replay: convert evidence into scenario steps (feeds scenario bank)

{"id":"iv-296","title":"Add CXDB-lite: run DAG + blob CAS for agent observability and recovery","description":"StrongDM's CXDB provides turn DAG, blob dedup, and typed payloads. Adopt the architecture as lightweight embedded infrastructure.

Implementation:
- SQLite tables: turns (id, parent_id, type, payload_hash, timestamp), blobs (hash, content), types (name, schema)
- Filesystem CAS fallback: .clavain/blobs/<blake3-hash> for large artifacts
- Typed event envelopes: autarch.turn.v1, autarch.toolcall.v1, autarch.scenario_result.v1
- O(1) fork: create new turn pointing to existing parent (DAG branching)
- Run manifests: .clavain/runs/<run_id>/manifest.json with pointers into DAG

MCP tools to expose:
- run:start, run:checkpoint, run:resume, run:fork
- run:inspect (returns DAG visualization or summary)
- run:log (append typed event to current run)

This is foundational for Attractor-mode pipelines, scenario recovery, and Bigend observability.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-11T13:05:12Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-02-27T08:10:29Z","work_type":"mutex"}

{"id":"iv-c2r","title":"Add scenario bank + satisfaction scoring to Gurgeh/Coldwine","description":"StrongDM's core validation: end-to-end user stories as holdout sets, scored by satisfaction (not boolean pass/fail).

Gurgeh side:
- New entity type: Scenario (intent, setup, steps, expected observations, rubric, risk tags)
- Store in .clavain/scenarios/dev/ (visible to implementation) and .clavain/scenarios/holdout/ (hidden)
- Scenario versioning alongside spec versioning
- Rubric-based scoring: deterministic checks + LLM-as-judge

Coldwine side:
- Scenario runner: execute scenarios, produce trajectory bundles
- Satisfaction scorer: compute satisfaction metric over trajectories
- Holdout isolation: implementation agents run in worktree WITHOUT .clavain/scenarios/holdout/
- Gate integration: refuse completion unless satisfaction >= threshold on holdouts

MCP tools to expose:
- scenario:create, scenario:list, scenario:run, scenario:score
- satisfaction:check (returns current score + pass/fail for gate)

This is the keystone capability — makes non-interactive execution defensible.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-11T13:05:07Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-02-27T08:10:29Z","work_type":"mutex"}

{"id":"iv-wbh","title":"Add Attractor-mode graph pipeline orchestration to Coldwine","description":"StrongDM's Attractor: non-interactive coding agent as a graph of phases with checkpoints, resume, fork. Extend Coldwine's task orchestration to support pipeline graphs.

Implementation:
- Pipeline definition format: YAML or DOT graph in .clavain/pipelines/<name>.yml
- Node contract: agent, inputs, tools_allowed, expected_outputs, gate_condition
- Edge evaluation: LLM evaluates natural-language conditions (Attractor pattern)
- Node outputs written to .clavain/runs/<run_id>/<node>/
- After each node: git commit on run branch, update run DAG (CXDB-lite)
- Resume from any checkpoint: /pipeline:resume <run_id> <node>
- Fork: /pipeline:fork <run_id> <node> --with 'new assumptions'

MCP tools to expose:
- pipeline:create, pipeline:run, pipeline:status
- pipeline:resume, pipeline:fork
- pipeline:visualize (returns graph with current execution state)

Depends on CXDB-lite for run state. Coldwine already has task graph primitives — this extends them with explicit phases, gates, and checkpoints.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-11T13:05:18Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-02-11T13:33:45Z","work_type":"mutex"}
